---
title: "Data card — mouse 16S microbiome"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
---

## How to load

Public CSVs from the Quadram Institute workshop (see URLs below). Join metadata and OTU table on sample ID; transpose to **samples x OTUs**. Details in [Day 2 lab Block A](../../exercises/mlss2026-lab-exercises.qmd#day-2--block-a-import--eda).

| File | URL |
|------|-----|
| Metadata | `https://raw.githubusercontent.com/quadram-institute-bioscience/datasciencegroup/main/4_machine_learning/mouse-16s/metadata.csv` |
| OTU table | `https://raw.githubusercontent.com/quadram-institute-bioscience/datasciencegroup/main/4_machine_learning/mouse-16s/otutab_raw.csv` |
| Taxonomy (optional) | `https://raw.githubusercontent.com/quadram-institute-bioscience/datasciencegroup/main/4_machine_learning/mouse-16s/taxonomy.csv` |

**Source repo:** [Quadram — ML with microbiome](https://github.com/quadram-institute-bioscience/datasciencegroup/tree/main/4_machine_learning) (Python workshop; we use **R + tidymodels**).

## Where we use this

- [Lab handbook](../../exercises/mlss2026-lab-exercises.qmd) — Day 2 Blocks A-D, Day 4 imbalance / imputation / metrics blocks
- Solutions: [EDA](../../exercises/solutions/day02-microbiome-eda.Rmd), [baseline workflow](../../exercises/solutions/day02-microbiome-baseline-workflow.Rmd), [tune CV](../../exercises/solutions/day02-microbiome-tune-cv.Rmd), Day 4 microbiome solutions

## Caveats

::: {.callout-important}
## Leakage warning

Samples are **repeated measures** per mouse (`Individual`). **Do not** use a random row split as your final answer.

- **Research-grade (default):** `group_vfold_cv` with `group = Individual`, or hold out whole mice.
- **Intro track:** one row per `Individual` (e.g. last timepoint only) — **document the simplification** on your data card.
:::

---

# Data card

## 1. Source

**Schloss mouse 16S** data distributed for the Quadram datasciencegroup machine-learning tutorial. Downloaded from GitHub raw CSV URLs (see table above). Not collected for this summer school; we reuse it for **high-dimensional count** workflows.

## 2. Outcome

- **`Label`**: factor **`Early`** vs **`Late`** (community shift over time in the experiment).

## 3. Predictors

- **OTU abundances** (thousands of columns after transpose); typically **log1p** transformed in recipes.
- Metadata columns (e.g. **`Individual`**, library size) — use for grouping and QC, not always as predictors.

## 4. Sample size

Roughly **~282** samples after aligning metadata and OTU table (exact *n* depends on filtering). **p >> n**. Library sizes and sparsity vary; EDA block explores this.

## 5. Leakage risks

- **Random row CV** when the same **mouse (`Individual`)** appears in train and test **inflates** performance.
- **Normalizing or filtering OTUs on the full table** before splitting leaks.
- **Intro track:** collapsing to one row per mouse simplifies analysis but changes the scientific question — state that explicitly.

## 6. Subgroups

- **`Individual`** (mouse ID) — primary grouping for honest resampling.
- Any experimental batch or time fields in metadata — check during EDA for stratification or subgroup metrics.

{{< include _footer.qmd >}}
